4.reason: [The function 'gets' is unsafe and has been removed from the C standard library since C11; many compilers no longer support it, leading to compilation errors. It should be replaced with 'fgets'.]

9.reason: [The loop declaration 'for (c[i - 1]; ...)' is invalid syntax in C. The first part of a for-loop must be a declaration or expression, but using just 'c[i - 1]' as a statement is not allowed and will cause a compilation error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop declaration 'for (c[i - 1]; ...)' is invalid syntax in C. The first part of a for-loop must be a declaration or expression, but using just 'c[i - 1]' as a statement is not allowed and will cause a compilation error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.25 , reason:  [The loop initialization 'c[i - 1]' does nothing; more critically, modifying 'c[i - 1]' inside the loop alters the original string, causing incorrect behavior and potential out-of-bound issues in subsequent iterations. Also, the loop condition logic is flawed鈥攊t should iterate from c[i-1]+1 up to c[i+1]-1 inclusive.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.25 , reason:  [Modifying c[i - 1] inside the loop alters the original string, which affects subsequent iterations and logic. Also, the loop condition and increment are logically flawed: it should use a temporary variable instead of modifying the input array.]
